concerns.analysis: The source PDF contains a meaningful agency logo ('NC Water Resources ENVIRONMENTAL QUALITY') at the top left of the first page. While an tag is present in the HTML output, the image manifest explicitly indicates that the base64 data for this image is corrupt ('valid': false, 'error': 'No module named \'PIL\''). Per the evaluation instructions, this means the image is broken and will not render, resulting in a failure for this check.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: NC Water Resources ENVIRONMENTAL QUALITY logo at the top left of page 1.
concerns.output_quote: The image manifest indicates "valid": false and "error": "No module named 'PIL'" for the logo image.

concerns.description: Header/footer elements retained in output (4): State of North Carolina | Environmental Quality | Water Reso; Page 2 of 4; Page 3 of 4
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=4. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML — the renderer failed to strip it. This is a structural accessibility issue.

The source document uses bold and underline formatting to emphasize the list item labels on pages 3 and 4 (e.g., 'Environmental Justice', 'Risk Classification', 'Costs', 'Landfills'). In the output JSON, this formatting is completely lost, and the text is rendered without any emphasis markers.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: List item prefixes are bold and underlined: 'Environmental Justice', 'Risk Classification', 'Not Applicable', 'Beneficial Reuse', 'Costs', 'Excavation', 'Groundwater Assessments', 'Landfills', 'Private Well Issues'
concerns.output_quote: The text lacks any emphasis formatting, e.g.: "Environmental Justice – One speaker...", "Risk Classification – A citizen..."

concerns.description: Mixed numbering types at same list level (1 list(s)): p1:letter+roman — possible sub-point flattening
concerns.analysis: flat_lists=1. Lists with mixed numbering types (numeric+letter or numeric+roman) at the same depth level suggest sub-points were flattened.
